Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

java.lang.Object

  • Method Details

    • assetId

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder assetId(String assetId)
      The ID of the alternative asset specified in the request.

    • assetId

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder assetId(Optional<String> assetId)
      The ID of the alternative asset specified in the request.

    • cashDistributionAllowed

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder cashDistributionAllowed(boolean cashDistributionAllowed)
      Indicates whether the asset allows cash distributions. If `TRUE` the alternative order request can specify `CASH` as the `distribution type`.

    • cashDistributionAllowed

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder cashDistributionAllowed(Optional<Boolean> cashDistributionAllowed)
      Indicates whether the asset allows cash distributions. If `TRUE` the alternative order request can specify `CASH` as the `distribution type`.

    • currentPrice

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder currentPrice(CurrentPrice currentPrice)
      The current price of one unit of the asset in USD.

    • currentPrice

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder currentPrice(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends CurrentPrice> currentPrice)
      The current price of one unit of the asset in USD.

    • cusip

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder cusip(String cusip)
      The CUSIP of the asset. It is a nine-character alphanumeric identifier used to uniquely identify securities, particularly in the United States and Canada.

      A true CUSIP consists of: - A six-character identifier for the issuer (company, municipality, etc.). - A two-character identifier for the type of security (equity, debt, etc.) and the specific issue within the issuer. - A single check digit.

      Many alternative investments assets do not have a true CUSIP. In such cases, the system generates a synthetic CUSIP when the asset is created.

    • cusip

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder cusip(Optional<String> cusip)
      The CUSIP of the asset. It is a nine-character alphanumeric identifier used to uniquely identify securities, particularly in the United States and Canada.

      A true CUSIP consists of: - A six-character identifier for the issuer (company, municipality, etc.). - A two-character identifier for the type of security (equity, debt, etc.) and the specific issue within the issuer. - A single check digit.

      Many alternative investments assets do not have a true CUSIP. In such cases, the system generates a synthetic CUSIP when the asset is created.

    • description

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder description(String description)
      A plain-text description of the asset, specified by the issuer.

    • description

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder description(Optional<String> description)
      A plain-text description of the asset, specified by the issuer.

    • dividendEnabled

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder dividendEnabled(boolean dividendEnabled)
      Indicates whether the asset distributes a dividend.

    • dividendEnabled

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder dividendEnabled(Optional<Boolean> dividendEnabled)
      Indicates whether the asset distributes a dividend.

    • initialSubscriptionAllowed

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder initialSubscriptionAllowed(boolean initialSubscriptionAllowed)
      Indicates whether the asset allows initial subscriptions to new investors.

    • initialSubscriptionAllowed

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder initialSubscriptionAllowed(Optional<Boolean> initialSubscriptionAllowed)
      Indicates whether the asset allows initial subscriptions to new investors.

    • isin

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder isin(String isin)
      The ISIN of the asset. It is a twelve-character alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify a security globally, as well as facilitate clearing, reporting, and settlement of trades defined by [ISO 6166](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Securities_Identification_Number).

      An ISIN consists of: - A two-letter country code. - A nine-character alphanumeric security identifier (National Securities Identifying Number or NSIN). - A single check digit.

      Not all alternative investment assets have an ISIN.

    • isin

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder isin(Optional<String> isin)
      The ISIN of the asset. It is a twelve-character alphanumeric code used to uniquely identify a security globally, as well as facilitate clearing, reporting, and settlement of trades defined by [ISO 6166](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Securities_Identification_Number).

      An ISIN consists of: - A two-letter country code. - A nine-character alphanumeric security identifier (National Securities Identifying Number or NSIN). - A single check digit.

      Not all alternative investment assets have an ISIN.

    • issuer

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder issuer(Issuer issuer)
      The issuer entity of the alternative investment asset.

    • issuer

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder issuer(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends Issuer> issuer)
      The issuer entity of the alternative investment asset.

    • issuingRegionCode

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder issuingRegionCode(String issuingRegionCode)
      The Unicode CLDR region code of the issuing region (ex: US).

    • issuingRegionCode

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder issuingRegionCode(Optional<String> issuingRegionCode)
      The Unicode CLDR region code of the issuing region (ex: US).

      The maximum number of shares allowed to purchase in a single order for this asset.

    • maximumSubscriptionQuantity

      The maximum number of shares allowed to purchase in a single order for this asset.

    • minimumInitialSubscriptionAmount

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder minimumInitialSubscriptionAmount(MinimumInitialSubscriptionAmount minimumInitialSubscriptionAmount)
      The minimum purchase amount required for an initial order of this asset. Value is in USD.

    • minimumInitialSubscriptionAmount

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder minimumInitialSubscriptionAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends MinimumInitialSubscriptionAmount> minimumInitialSubscriptionAmount)
      The minimum purchase amount required for an initial order of this asset. Value is in USD.

    • minimumSubscriptionQuantity

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder minimumSubscriptionQuantity(int minimumSubscriptionQuantity)
      The minimum number of shares allowed to purchase in a single order for this asset.

    • minimumSubscriptionQuantity

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder minimumSubscriptionQuantity(Optional<Integer> minimumSubscriptionQuantity)
      The minimum number of shares allowed to purchase in a single order for this asset.

    • minimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder minimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount(MinimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount minimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount)
      The minimum purchase amount required for subsequent orders of this asset. Value is in USD.

    • minimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder minimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends MinimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount> minimumSubsequentSubscriptionAmount)
      The minimum purchase amount required for subsequent orders of this asset. Value is in USD.

    • name

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder name(String name)
      The system-generated resource name of the alternative investment asset. Format: assets/{asset}/alternativeInvestment Note that `{asset}` will be the `asset_id` value.

    • name

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder name(Optional<String> name)
      The system-generated resource name of the alternative investment asset. Format: assets/{asset}/alternativeInvestment Note that `{asset}` will be the `asset_id` value.

    • nonRetirementEligible

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder nonRetirementEligible(boolean nonRetirementEligible)
      Indicates whether non-retirement accounts (ex: individual) are eligible to hold the alternative investment asset. - If `TRUE` then non-retirement accounts are eligible to hold the asset. - If `FALSE` then non-retirement accounts are not eligible to hold the asset.

    • nonRetirementEligible

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder nonRetirementEligible(Optional<Boolean> nonRetirementEligible)
      Indicates whether non-retirement accounts (ex: individual) are eligible to hold the alternative investment asset. - If `TRUE` then non-retirement accounts are eligible to hold the asset. - If `FALSE` then non-retirement accounts are not eligible to hold the asset.

    • perShareFeeAmount

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder perShareFeeAmount(PerShareFeeAmount perShareFeeAmount)
      The fee amount charged per share of the Alternative Investment in USD. This is included in current price.

    • perShareFeeAmount

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder perShareFeeAmount(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<? extends PerShareFeeAmount> perShareFeeAmount)
      The fee amount charged per share of the Alternative Investment in USD. This is included in current price.

    • preIpoCompanyId

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder preIpoCompanyId(String preIpoCompanyId)
      The ID of the Pre IPO Company associated with the Alternative Investment. Not all Alternative Investments are associated with a Pre IPO Company.

    • preIpoCompanyId

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder preIpoCompanyId(Optional<String> preIpoCompanyId)
      The ID of the Pre IPO Company associated with the Alternative Investment. Not all Alternative Investments are associated with a Pre IPO Company.

    • recordState

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder recordState(RecordState recordState)
      Indicates the current state of the asset record. The record state identifies which record types the asset allows.

    • recordState

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder recordState(Optional<? extends RecordState> recordState)
      Indicates the current state of the asset record. The record state identifies which record types the asset allows.

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder redeemable(boolean redeemable)
      Indicates whether the asset is allowed to be redeemed.

      Indicates whether the asset is allowed to be redeemed.

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder redemptionFrequency(RedemptionFrequency redemptionFrequency)
      Indicates how frequently an account can redeem the asset.

      Indicates how frequently an account can redeem the asset.

    • reinvestmentDistributionAllowed

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder reinvestmentDistributionAllowed(boolean reinvestmentDistributionAllowed)
      Indicates whether the asset allows reinvestment distributions.

      If `TRUE` the alternative order request can specify `REINVESTMENT` as the `distribution type`.

    • reinvestmentDistributionAllowed

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder reinvestmentDistributionAllowed(Optional<Boolean> reinvestmentDistributionAllowed)
      Indicates whether the asset allows reinvestment distributions.

      If `TRUE` the alternative order request can specify `REINVESTMENT` as the `distribution type`.

    • retirementEligible

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder retirementEligible(boolean retirementEligible)
      Indicates whether retirement accounts (ex: IRA) are eligible to hold the asset. - If `TRUE` then retirement accounts are eligible to hold the asset. - If `FALSE` then retirement accounts are not eligible to hold the asset.

    • retirementEligible

      public AlternativeInvestment.Builder retirementEligible(Optional<Boolean> retirementEligible)
      Indicates whether retirement accounts (ex: IRA) are eligible to hold the asset. - If `TRUE` then retirement accounts are eligible to hold the asset. - If `FALSE` then retirement accounts are not eligible to hold the asset.
